question: For the `Book` class (from a previous problem), overload the output stream operator `<<` to allow printing `Book` objects directly to `std::cout` in a formatted way (e.g., "Title: [title], Author: [author], ISBN: [isbn]").

test_cases.expected:
Book 1 details (inefficient string construction):
Title: The Lord of the Rings, Author: J.R.R. Tolkien, ISBN: 978-0618260274

Book 2 details (inefficient string construction):
Title: Dune, Author: Frank Herbert, ISBN: 978-0441013593

errors: The overloaded output stream operator constructs a temporary `std::string` object to hold the entire formatted output before streaming it to `std::ostream`. This is less efficient than directly streaming each component (title, author, ISBN) to the output stream, as it involves unnecessary memory allocation and copying for the temporary string.
